input_row.title: Consider external secret storage
input_row.description: Consider the use of an external secrets storage and management system, instead of using Kubernetes Secrets directly, if you have more complex secret management needs. Ensure the solution requires authentication to access secrets, has auditing of access to and use of secrets, and encrypts secrets. Some solutions also make it easier to rotate secrets.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes supports secrets as first-class objects, but care needs to be taken to ensure that access to secrets is carefully limited. Using an external secrets provider can ease the management of access to secrets, especially where secrests are used across both Kubernetes and non-Kubernetes environments. Impact: None
input_row.remediation: Refer to the secrets management options offered by your cloud provider or a third-party secrets management solution. Default Value: By default, no external secret management is configured. References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v2- identity-management#im-7-eliminate-unintended-credential-exposure

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Navigate to the Azure portal.
   - Go to "Kubernetes services" from the left-hand menu or search for "Kubernetes services" in the search bar.

2. Select the AKS cluster you want to review.
   - Click on the specific AKS cluster resource.

3. Review the Secrets Management implementation for the selected AKS cluster.
   - Check whether Kubernetes Secrets are being used and identify if any external secrets management solution is configured. 

4. Verify if there is an external secrets provider in use.
   - Look in the "Integrations" or "Configuration" tabs for any configurations related to external secrets management. This may include references to Azure Key Vault or third-party solutions like HashiCorp Vault.

5. Evaluate the authentication, auditing, and encryption settings of the external secrets solution.
   - Ensure that the external secrets provider allows for authenticated access.
   - Confirm that access logs and auditing of the secret usage are enabled.
   - Check if the secrets are being encrypted at rest and in transit as per the policies of the external secrets storage solution.

6. Document your findings based on the above steps.
   - Compare against best practices and organizational security policies to determine if any adjustments are needed.

7. Confirm compliance and prepare a report of findings along with any recommended remediation steps. 
   - If any deviations or lack of external secret management is found, recommend transitioning to a secure external secrets management solution, noting the benefits regarding secret rotation and access management.
